valuationMethod: CIF with configurable vehicle duty and levy pack; user FOB, CIF, purchase price, and valuation seeds are supported.

ageRules.message: Nigeria prohibits used passenger vehicle imports above 12 years. Treat an older vehicle as compliance-ineligible until a licensed clearing agent confirms otherwise.

steeringRules.message: No steering-side gate is modelled in this pack.

inspectionRules.label: Customs and standards inspection
inspectionRules.message: Budget for customs examination, terminal checks, and local compliance processing.

taxComponents.label: Passenger vehicle import duty
taxComponents.notes: Seeded as configurable car-duty pack, not a permanent hardcoded assumption.

taxComponents.label: Configurable vehicle levy
taxComponents.notes: Keep this component editable because vehicle levy treatment can change by policy pack.

taxComponents.label: Configurable 4% FOB implementation component
taxComponents.notes: Disabled by default so the applicable Nigerian rate pack can be published without a code change.

documents: Form M / PAAR documents where applicable

sourceNotes.name: Nigeria Customs Service - import guidelines
sourceNotes.note: Used for documentation and compliance framing.

sourceNotes.name: Nigeria Customs Service FOB implementation press release
sourceNotes.note: Tracks 2025 FOB charge discussions so the rule pack remains versionable.

disclaimers: This is an estimate, not an official customs assessment.
Vehicle duty and levy components are intentionally stored in editable rule data because Nigeria vehicle policy packs can change.
Older-than-12-year passenger vehicles should be treated as compliance-ineligible until confirmed by a licensed agent.

faqs.question: Can I calculate Nigeria if I only know the car price?
faqs.answer: Yes. The calculator treats purchase price as a FOB-style estimate, adds freight and marine insurance, then calculates on CIF. You can switch to FOB or CIF mode when you have cleaner shipping documents.

faqs.question: Why is there a Nigeria rule-pack warning?
faqs.answer: Nigeria has had policy movement around FOB-based charges and vehicle levies. The calculator keeps those layers editable and source-dated so a new pack can be published without changing code.

copy.intro: Estimate the real cost to land a used or new vehicle in Nigeria through Lagos ports, including customs taxes, practical clearing costs, registration, storage risk, and inland movement.
copy.hiddenCosts: Users often miss terminal delivery orders, scanning or examination fees, local registration, demurrage after free days, and FX spread between the quote date and clearing date.
